creator: yaldabaoth@geocities.com
text:
To follow up on previous reports:

The problem still persists in v 3.3 (AND 4.0). The fix is the same as in
all rpevious versions, but I will provide a diff specifically for the
3.3 version of wd.c:

125a126,127
> #define WDCAP_LBA 0x02
>
1942,1943c1944,1946
<   if (wp->wdp_cylinders == 16383 &&
<       du->dk_dd.d_secperunit < wp->wdp_lbasize) {
---
>   if ((wp->wdp_cylinders == 16383 ||
>        wp->wdp_capability & WDCAP_LBA) &&
>        du->dk_dd.d_secperunit < wp->wdp_lbasize) {

(The same logic applies to patching 4.0, though specific line numbers
may be different.)

Also, the following is a proposed patch for the ata-disk.c code. I have
not had a chance to test this change, but the logic is identical to the
logic in wd.c. As the ata code for disk drives seems to be strongly
influenced by the original wd.c code, the same logic should work:

165,168c165,169
<   if (adp->cylinders == 16383 &&
<       adp->total_secs < adp->ata_parm->lbasize) {
<       adp->total_secs = adp->ata_parm->lbasize;
<       adp->cylinders = adp->total_secs/(adp->heads*adp->sectors);
---
>   if ((adp->cylinders == 16383 ||
>        adp->ata_parm->lbaflag) &&
>        adp->total_secs < adp->ata_parm->lbasize) {
>        adp->total_secs = adp->ata_parm->lbasize;
>        adp->cylinders = adp->total_secs/(adp->heads*adp->sectors);

A note about a statement in ata-disk.h. The definition of the ata_params
structure states that lbaflag is always 1. This is in contradiction of
the ATA-2 standard, which states that a 1 in this paritcular bit field
must indicate that  the lbasize field gives a valid measure of available
space. Thus, despite the statement in ata-disk.h the patch above should
only use lbasize if it is a valid value.

Andrew Sherrod
